name: nc
description: Arbitrary TCP and UDP connections and listens

options.description: Forces nc to use IPv4 addresses only
Forces nc to use IPv6 addresses only

options.name: -A
options.description: Set SO_RECV_ANYIF on socket

options.name: -b
options.description: Specifies the interface to bind the socket to

options.name: -c
options.description: Send CRLF as line-ending

options.name: -D
options.description: Enable debugging on the socket

options.name: -C
options.description: Force nc not to use cellular data context

options.name: -d
options.description: Do not attempt to read from stdin

options.name: -h
options.description: Prints out nc help

options.name: -i
options.description: Specifies a delay time interval between lines of text sent and received

options.name: -G
options.description: TCP connection timeout in seconds

options.name: -H
options.description: Initial TCP keep alive timeout in seconds

options.name: -I
options.description: Interval for repeating TCP keep alive timeout in seconds

options.name: -J
options.description: Number of times to repeat TCP keep alive packets

options.name: -k
options.description: Force nc to stay listening for another connection after its current connection is completed

options.name: -l
options.description: Used to specify that nc should listen for an incoming connection rather than initiate a connection to a remote host

options.name: -L
options.description: Number of prbes to send to the peer before declaring that the peer is not reachable

options.name: -n
options.description: Do not do any DNS or service lookups on any specified addresses, hostnames or ports

options.name: -p
options.description: Specifies the source port nc should use, subject to privilege restrictions and availability

options.name: -r
options.description: Specifies that source and/or destination ports should be chosen randomly instead of sequentially within a range or in the order that the system assigns them

options.name: -s
options.description: Specifies the IP of the interface which is used to send the packets

options.name: -t
options.description: Causes nc to send RFC 854 DON'T and WON'T responses to RFC 854 DO and WILL requests

options.name: -U
options.description: Specifies to use Unix Domain Sockets

options.name: -u
options.description: Use UDP instead of the default option of TCP

options.name: -v
options.description: Have nc give more verbose output

options.name: -w
options.description: If a connection and stdin are idle for more than timeout seconds, then the connection is silently closed

options.name: -X
options.description: Requests that nc should use the specified protocol when talking to the proxy server

options.name: -x
options.description: Requests that nc should connect to hostname using a proxy at proxy_address and port

options.name: -z
options.description: Specifies that nc should just scan for listening daemons, without sending any data to them

options.name: --apple-delegate-pid
options.description: Requests that nc should delegate the socket for the specified PID

options.name: --apple-delegate-uuid
options.description: Requests that nc should delegate the socket for the specified UUID

options.name: --apple-ext-bk-idle
options.description: Requests that nc marks its socket for extended background idle time when the process becomes suspended

options.name: --apple-nowakefromsleep
options.description: When the parameter n is greater than 0, requests that nc marks its socket to exlude the local port from the list of opened ports that is queried by drivers when the system goes to sleep args

options.name: --ecn
options.description: Requests that nc marks to use the socket option TCP_ECN_MODE to set the ECN mode
